page: Amazon Web Services m8i.metal-48xl is a general-purpose bare-metal server powered by Intel Xeon 6975P-C processors. It features an x86_64 architecture with 192 vCPUs across 96 physical cores, operating at a clock speed of 3.9 GHz. The system is equipped with 768.0 GB of system memory, yielding a balanced allocation of 4.0 GB per core. It has a massive L3 cache of 480 MB, alongside 48 KB L1d and 2 MB L2 caches. This server does not include local storage or dedicated GPUs, but it provides a baseline network bandwidth of 75 Gbps.

Benchmark data indicates top-tier multi-core processing capabilities. The server achieves top-10% performance in stress-ng multi-core CPU tests, PassMark CPU scores, and Geekbench multi-core evaluations. Memory bandwidth is also highly rated, reaching top-tier status in both small-block cached and large-block uncached read/write operations. However, single-core performance shows some variance; while Geekbench single-core scores rank in the top 10%, stress-ng single-core CPU performance falls into the middle 50% average category.

The CPU architecture includes advanced instruction sets such as Intel Advanced Vector Extensions (AVX-512) and Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX), which support top-tier performance in PassMark extended instructions and encryption tests. These extensions also enable top-tier LLM inference prompt processing speeds for 135M, 7B, and 70B models. However, text generation speeds for the 135M model drop to the middle 50% average. The lack of local storage requires external network-attached storage, which is supported by the 75 Gbps network interface.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the bare-metal configuration offers high resource density and direct hardware access, maximizing the return on compute-heavy workloads without hypervisor overhead. This server is targeted at multi-threaded applications, database operations, software compilation, static web serving, and CPU-based machine learning inference.
description: Amazon Web Services m8i.metal-48xl is a general-purpose bare-metal server featuring 192 vCPUs across 96 physical Intel Xeon 6975P-C cores running at 3.9 GHz. It is equipped with 768.0 GB of memory, providing a balanced 4.0 GB per core ratio, and features a 480 MB L3 cache. The server lacks local storage and dedicated GPUs but offers a 75 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. Benchmark results place this server in the top tier for multi-core CPU performance, memory bandwidth, and database operations. It supports advanced instruction sets like AVX-512 and AMX, facilitating top-tier performance in cryptography and LLM prompt processing. While single-core stress-ng performance is average, its massive parallel processing capacity makes it effective for multi-threaded workloads. This bare-metal instance is targeted at database operations, software compilation, static web serving, and CPU-based machine learning inference.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m8i.metal-48xl is a bare-metal server with 192 vCPUs, 768 GB RAM, and a 3.9 GHz Intel Xeon CPU, delivering top-tier multi-core performance and 75 Gbps network bandwidth for demanding enterprise workloads.

tagline: A bare-metal platform featuring 192 vCPUs, 768 GB RAM, and advanced matrix extensions for high-throughput multi-threaded computing and database operations.
